items.title: Tongan women 'auctioned off' for marriage in China

children.content: The ABC has spoken to multiple Tongan women who have been lured into an arranged marriage syndicate operating in China, with allegations of passports being confiscated and tens of thousands of dollars changing hands.

gallery.altText: Close-up of a woman wearing a face mask.

thumbnail.altText: Close-up of a woman wearing a face mask.

items.title: Ex-ASIO source denied visa to give evidence at Bondi royal commission

children.content: The man who previously warned ASIO about the Bondi gunmen won't be able to give sensitive and classified evidence at the Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion.

image.altText: A man wearing a cap stands by a window. He is looking to the side past the camera with a serious expression.

items.title: The 'fairy gold dust passport' more Australians are chasing

children.content: Australians are racing to secure Irish citizenship, with new figures showing applications have more than doubled in the past three years as people seek the right to live, work and study across the EU.

image.altText: A family of four taking a selfie infront of the colloseum in Rome

items.title: 'Nervous' investors retreat from housing market as downturn broadens

children.content: Australia's property market has entered a downturn after both house and unit prices fell for the first time in more than three years, bringing an end to the recent housing boom.

items.title: Inside CPAC London: Charlatans, conspiracy theories and loud snoring

children.content: In 10 years at the ABC, I've had some strange experiences. I can now add last week's CPAC conference in London to the list, and it's going somewhere near the top, says Riley Stuart in London.

image.altText: A woman and a man speaking.

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

items.title: China demands Australia stop 'inciting confrontation' over South China Sea

children.content: China's Ministry of Foreign Affairs has demanded Australia stop "exaggerating tensions and inciting confrontation" over the South China Sea after Penny Wong warned Beijing against "destabilising" conduct in the region.

image.altText: Penny Wong and Wang Yi shake hands.

items.title: Modi is 'used to winning': He's facing an unprecedented threat to his power

children.content: The student who started a youth uprising across India is fast becoming the biggest challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's 12-year reign.

gallery.altText: Young protesters on a city street, some holding India flags.

thumbnail.altText: Young protesters on a city street, some holding India flags.

items.title: Labor conference to debate gas and gambling

children.content: Anthony Albanese may have been a keen participant in robust conference debates in years gone by but is now using his authority to enforce a much more controlled process.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese stands in front of an Australian flag in a grey suit and blue tie looking serious

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

items.title: Meet the Australian multi-millionaire pardoned by Trump

children.content: Chris Wade was arrested by the Secret Service in Dallas in the early 2000s. It took him on a journey from FBI hacker to Mar-a-Lago and eventually the White House before he came back to Australia to buy his favourite pub.

image.altText: Two men standing with Donald Trump in the middle.

items.title: How we got to Glasgow 2026 and what the Commonwealth Games will look like

children.content: The bumpy road to Glasgow 2026 has produced a Commonwealth Games that will look a little different than the ones that came before.

articles.title: Your guide to every day of the Commonwealth Games in Australian time

children.content: With 10 sports crammed into 10 full days of action in Glasgow, it may be hard to keep track of what's on and when at the Commonwealth Games. So, here's the full schedule of every day of the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games with all times and days in AEST.

image.altText: A basketballer spins a ball on his finger, a boxer poses, a weightlifter holds up a barbell, and a wheelchair basketballer.

articles.title: Kennedy arrives early to lead Australia into Glasgow opening ceremony

children.content: Reigning Commonwealth Games gold medallists Nina Kennedy and Tim Hodge will carry the Australian flag and King's baton at the Glasgow 2026 opening ceremony.

image.altText: Nina Kennedy holds the Australian flag behind her.

gallery.altText: Swimmer Duncan Scott with 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games mascots Clyde and Finnie.

thumbnail.altText: Swimmer Duncan Scott with 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games mascots Clyde and Finnie.

items.title: French modelling scout with links to Epstein found dead, prosecutors say

children.content: A French modelling scout with close ties to the late US financier Jeffrey Epstein has been found dead at his home outside Paris, prosecutors say.

image.altText: A man wearing a black tshirt is smiling as he holds a cigar.

items.title: How One Nation's winning over the bush

children.content: One Nation has some of its strongest support in outback Australia and recent polling shows it could win over 30 regional seats. So what's winning over regional Australians when it comes to Pauline Hanson's party?

image.altText: A red-haired woman is pictured on a billboard of a pool with the message $13 million Cooee Bay Pool delivered

items.title: US and Saudi Arabia set to unveil nuclear power deal, media reports say

items.title: Minister says Australian IS-linked 'Doctor Jihad' can 'rot' in Iraqi jail

children.content: Perth paediatrician Tareq Kamleh went to Syria to join the Islamic State group in 2015 and has, until now, been presumed dead.

image.altText: Dr Tareq Kamleh

items.title: How Nintendo's tariff hike ended with angry gamers and a lawsuit

children.content: Nintendo has argued it has no duty to pass its refunds onto US consumers. It's the latest in a lengthy tariff fight.

items.title: You reckon you know the Logies? Try our quiz

items.title: 'Unheard and uncared for': Children in state care exploited by adults

children.content: The ABC has spoken with experts, advocates and residential care workers as part of a 19-month investigation into the sexual exploitation of children in state care.

items.title: AFL’s first publicly gay player calls for respect in Crows-Pies match

items.title: Animal experts divided about Barnaby Joyce's TV dog discipline

children.content: One Nation MP Barnaby Joyce is back in the spotlight after some harsh words to his dog during a live TV interview, but experts are divided about what works when it comes to pulling a canine into line.

items.title: Anxiety as Australia issues new travel advice for Middle East countries

children.content: As conflict between the United States and Iran escalates once again, the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade has issued a warning to "reconsider" travel to several more countries in the region.

articles.title: How one of Iran's 'Axis' allies is threatening to widen the war

children.content: The Houthi rebels, described as one of Iran's "merchants of chaos", have threatened to widen the conflict in the Middle East by declaring a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia. Here's what could happen next.

articles.title: Hegseth outlines cost of Iran war at Senate hearing

children.content: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is being questioned on the cost of the war against Iran, his idea of testosterone screening for troops, and the number of service people who have died.

image.altText: Pete Hegseth seated, speaking into a small microphone.

items.title: Data company urged to be 'good' after water plan slip reveals intentions

children.content: A Tasmanian farmer has urged a data centre builder to "be a good corporate citizen" after it accidentally revealed a plan to tap into a water source earmarked for agriculture for cooling purposes at one of its facilities.

image.altText: A lot in a residential area with a big building on it.

items.title: Court date set for trial of captured Venezuelan president Maduro

children.content: US commandos captured Nicolás Maduro and his wife Cilia Flores in January this year and took them to New York, where they now face a number of charges including narcoterrorism.

image.altText: Protesters on a New York street holding signs saying 'Free President Maduro'.

items.title: Opposition promises to build new West Gippsland hospital if elected

children.content: The Victorian Coalition pledges $850 million to build a new West Gippsland hospital if it wins the November election.

items.title: Hanson labels domestic violence 'two-way street'

items.title: Rural suburb flagged as home for proposed $40b 'hyperscale' data centre

children.content: A proposed massive new data centre would require more than six times the total demand of the Darwin and Katherine electricity grids to operate and be powered by gas piped direct from the Northern Territory's Beetaloo Basin.

articles.title: 'We do not want to highlight this yet': AI data centre's FAQ slip-up

children.content: Data centre builder Firmus posted a statement, since removed, indicating its intention to get some of its cooling water from the state-owned irrigation system, adding "but we do not want to highlight this yet".

articles.title: AI companies 'stole' Australian culture, news bosses say

children.content: The heads of the ABC, SBS, Nine and News Corp say AI companies should strike licensing deals rather than freely train on Australian news, warning journalism is a public good that must be protected.

items.title: Lewis breaks Australian 100m record, beats five-time Olympic champ

children.content: Torrie Lewis shows she is hitting her straps ahead of the Commonwealth Games by beating her own record mark during a meet in Slovakia.

articles.title: Cameron McEvoy has lived a life between trips to Glasgow

articles.title: Fox sisters combine in 'dream team' for gold medals at World Championships
